name: directory-profile-engine
description: One master profile, every directory consistent: inventory where the practice is (and should be) listed, write master profile copy in the clinician's voice, cut per-directory variants, and check name-address-phone consistency across all of them — then the clinician pastes each update in themselves. No PHI, no superlatives, no invented credentials; complements local-seo-engine, which handles the search-presence layer.

steps.title: Directory inventory: {project}
steps.description: Map where the {project} practice is currently listed and which directories genuinely matter for its population and location — the general business surfaces (search and maps listings) and the therapy-specific directories the practice already uses or plausibly should. Base the inventory ONLY on what the practice actually reports; where the current listing state is unknown, write [CHECK: clinician confirms current listing + login]. Do not invent a listing, a ranking, or traffic data. Output: the inventory table — directory, current state or [CHECK], and why it matters for this practice or why it can be skipped.

steps.title: Master profile copy: {project}
steps.description: Write the master profile for the {project} practice in the clinician's own voice: who they are, who they serve, how they work, logistics (location, telehealth, hours, contact), and what a first session is like. Long enough to cut down from. Every credential and service must be a real, verifiable practice fact or [CHECK]; no superlatives, no rankings, no outcome language, no filler warmth. This is the single source every directory variant derives from.

steps.title: Per-directory variants + consistency checklist: {project}
steps.description: Cut the master into per-directory variants for each surface the inventory kept — respecting each directory's customary length and structure, never padding or upgrading a claim beyond the master. Then produce the consistency checklist: the practice's canonical name, address, phone, and hours written once, with a checkbox row per directory so the clinician can verify every listing shows the identical details. Inconsistent basics quietly undermine a practice's findability — say that plainly in the checklist header.

steps.title: Honesty review: {project} directory package
steps.description: Review the inventory, master, variants, and checklist against the Shaula honesty rules. Confirm every variant is derivable from the master with nothing added; every credential and service is real or [CHECK]; no superlative or outcome claim anywhere; the canonical details appear identically in every variant. Hold until clean.

steps.title: Human gate — clinician updates each directory: {project}
steps.description: HUMAN GATE: the clinician logs into each directory with their own credentials, pastes the approved variant, and ticks the consistency checklist row. The office never holds directory logins and never edits a listing itself. Record which directories were updated and the date, so the next refresh starts from truth.
